channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Marcus Rashford's Goal Validated by Offside Rule Analysis in France vs England Match
item.description: England dominated France in a 4-0 first-half lead during the 2026 World Cup third-place play-off, with Bukayo Saka scoring twice alongside goals from Declan Rice and Ezri Konsa. The match sparked deba...

item.title: FIFA Introduces Championship Rings for 2026 World Cup Winners, Limited Editions for Fans
item.description: FIFA has announced a groundbreaking tradition for the 2026 World Cup final, where winning players will receive customized championship rings alongside the traditional trophy and gold medals. This mark...

item.title: Balancing Retirement and University Costs: A Financial Planning Guide
item.description: The article explores the financial dilemma of parents considering delaying retirement to fund their daughter's university education. It outlines a practical approach to estimate the additional years o...

item.title: Springbok Coach Rassie Erasmus Evaluates Vusi Moyo's Debut Performance Against Wales
item.description: Springbok coach Rassie Erasmus provided a measured assessment of Vusi Moyo's debut performance as the team's flyhalf in their 43-0 victory over Wales. Moyo, a 20-year-old Junior Springbok, made his in...

item.title: SADC bloc to enforce 28th meeting's resolutions for regional peace and stability
item.description: The Southern African Development Community (SADC) bloc has been assigned the critical responsibility of implementing resolutions from the 28th meeting aimed at ensuring regional peace and stability. T...

item.title: Tornado Warning Briefly Issued for Southern Ontario Communities
item.description: A tornado warning was briefly issued for parts of southern Ontario on Saturday, July 18, 2026, but was later lifted by Environment Canada. The warning, which came into effect around 12:20 p.m., was tr...

item.title: Kalinga Farmers Advised to Delay Palay Planting Amid Water Shortages
item.description: Farmers in Kalinga reliant on the Upper Chico River Irrigation System (UCRIS) have been advised by the National Irrigation Administration (NIA) to delay palay planting due to declining water levels in...

item.title: Escalating Conflict: Iran and U.S. Continue Strikes Amid Peace Deal Abandonment
item.description: The article details the ongoing escalation of violence between Iran and the United States, with both nations intensifying strikes on each other's infrastructure. Iran has suspended its commitments und...

item.title: Nigerian and Filipina Wife Arrested for Possessing P3.4 Million Shabu in Cavite
item.description: A Nigerian national and his Filipina spouse were apprehended by authorities in Cavite after a drug raid uncovered approximately P3.4 million worth of shabu (methamphetamine). The incident, reported on...

item.title: Taylor Farms Recalls Iceberg Lettuce Across 27 States Over Cyclospora Contamination Risk
item.description: Taylor Farms has initiated a recall of iceberg lettuce products distributed to 27 U.S. states following concerns about potential cyclospora contamination. The parasite, which causes severe gastrointes...

item.title: Karen Nyamu Shares Her Preference for Men with Stories Over Wealth
item.description: Nominated senator Karen Nyamu has sparked discussions about her views on relationships, emphasizing that money alone cannot win her affection. In a post inspired by the Ol Kalou by-elections, she argu...

item.title: Springboks dominate Wales 43-0 as debutants shine in Durban clash
item.description: The South African national rugby team, the Springboks, delivered a dominant performance against Wales in their Nations Championship match at Kings Park Stadium in Durban, securing a comprehensive 43-0...

item.title: Tanzania promotes unity and youth empowerment on Mandela Day
item.description: Tanzania is celebrating Mandela Day by emphasizing the importance of unity, volunteerism, and youth empowerment across the nation. The event, held on July 18, 2026, highlights the legacy of Nelson Man...

item.title: First Nations winemaker blends tradition and sustainability in Margaret River
item.description: Florence Drummond, a Torres Strait-born First Nations woman, has transitioned from a mining executive role to establishing a wine label in Margaret River, Western Australia. Her venture goes beyond co...

item.title: Argentina eye World Cup defense against Spain in 2026
item.description: Argentina are on the brink of becoming just the third team in World Cup history to successfully defend their title, with a showdown against European champions Spain looming. The article highlights Arg...

item.title: Long-term study links neighborhood conditions to women's cardiovascular risk over time
item.description: A long-term study led by the Harvard Pilgrim Health Care Institute followed 1,200 women in eastern Massachusetts for over two decades, revealing that living in disadvantaged neighborhoods significantl...

item.title: Volvo tests conversation quality as voice control faces challenges
item.description: The article discusses how Volvo is shifting its focus from traditional car specifications to evaluating the quality of conversations in its vehicles. Voice-controlled systems, once seen as a convenien...

item.title: Nashik woman killed by ex-lover in suspected murder-suicide case
item.description: A 20-year-old woman was stabbed to death by her ex-lover in Nashik, Maharashtra, on Friday night. The accused, who had met the victim earlier that evening, allegedly attacked her multiple times near a...

item.title: West Hollywood Water Main Rupture: Progress Made, Sunset Strip Still Closed
item.description: A major water main rupture in West Hollywood caused a massive sinkhole and flooded neighborhoods, damaging over 150 vehicles and requiring extensive repairs. The 100-year-old pipe, which burst on July...

item.title: UDA MP Linked to Violence and Fake Plates in Ol Kalou Probe
item.description: The article details how Kiambaa MP John Njuguna Wanjiku, known as Ka-Wanjiku, denied involvement in the Ol Kalou by-election violence despite his black Toyota Land Cruiser being linked to masked attac...
